title: tylophora indica var. glabra
intro: Tylophora indica var. glabra is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the 'Indian ipecac' or 'tylophora'. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as tylophorine and tylophorinol. The health benefits of Tylophora indica var. glabra include its potential to manage respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of various infections. Herbal preparations of Tylophora indica var. glabra include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally or used topically to treat various health conditions.
